sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Bryan, presents with a history of low back pain that started approximately 2 hours after raking leaves in his yard.
He describes the pain as a tightening sensation in his back and a tingling sensation in his right leg.
The pain is exacerbated by standing and flexion, but relieved by lying down and sitting.
He has had similar episodes in the past, with the last episode occurring 3 years ago, which resolved after a day.
He has tried ice and acetaminophen (2 Advils) with no relief.
His partner reports similar symptoms, particularly with standing.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's dorsiflexion is normal, and pulses are equal in all extremities.
The patient experiences pain on flexion, palpation, and extension, particularly around the L5 area.
The pain is exacerbated by standing.
Test Results: X-ray of the low back, normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely sprained his low back, which is causing his symptoms of low back pain and tingling sensation in his right leg.
Problem list: Low back sprain; tingling sensation in right leg; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally once daily for pain relief.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help improve mobility and reduce pain.
I want the patient to rest his back and avoid heavy lifting or bending for the next 2 weeks.
If the symptoms do not improve, I want the patient to follow up with me and we will consider an MRI.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 2 weeks to re-evaluate his symptoms and determine if further testing is necessary.
